ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for fish salad with egg and multiple toppings; 'salad food product' (FOODON_00002809) used as the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Salads) is the closest defensible category for a composed fish-and-egg salad with mixed vegetable toppings. No exact code exists for this customisable dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this customisable composed salad dish.

preparation_method: no cooking required for salad base

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g, representing a full plate of mixed greens topped with fish, egg, four vegetable toppings, and dressing as typically served in a Singapore café or hawker setting.

nutrient_source.title: Fish salad with egg (4 toppings) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Fish salad with egg (4 toppings)' (cr_id F08010026). All per-100g macros and micronutrients reported by HPB are taken verbatim from this canonical record. Saturated fat is not separately reported by HPB for this entry and is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous mixed salad with fish and egg components.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed salad with protein (fish/egg) and non-starchy vegetables
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific customisable dish. GI estimated at approximately 32 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous category of mixed green salad with protein (fish and egg) and non-starchy vegetable toppings. The dominant carbohydrate contributors are non-starchy vegetables and a small amount of dressing; the protein and fat content from fish and egg further blunt the glycaemic response. Glycaemic load per 300 g serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (32 × 9.72) / 100 ≈ 3.1 g, which is very low.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content at 347 mg per 100 g; a 300 g serving delivers approximately 1,041 mg sodium, representing roughly 45% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request dressing on the side, avoid high-sodium dressings and pickled toppings, and limit portion size to manage sodium intake within daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate content (3.24 g per 100 g), low estimated GI (~32), and 1.78 g dietary fibre per 100 g combine to produce a very low glycaemic load of approximately 3.1 per standard serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; patients should avoid sweet dressings, limit starchy toppings such as corn, and confirm fish is not battered or breaded to preserve the low glycaemic profile.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (estimated ~1.2 g per 100 g), moderate total fat (5.57 g per 100 g) predominantly from fish and egg, and high dietary fibre relative to calorie density support a heart-healthy profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should choose oily fish (e.g., salmon or mackerel) for omega-3 benefit, select vinaigrette over creamy dressings to minimise saturated fat, and monitor sodium from dressing to stay within cardiovascular sodium targets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Four vegetable toppings increase antioxidant diversity; fish and egg provide lean protein; low saturated fat and absence of deep-frying support an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should choose oily fish for omega-3 anti-inflammatory benefit, avoid creamy high-saturated-fat dressings, and be aware that nightshade toppings (tomato, bell pepper, eggplant) may aggravate symptoms in solanine-sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Salad with Egg (4 Toppings) is a freshly assembled Western-style salad dish served in Singapore cafés and hawker settings. It consists of freshly prepared fish — typically grilled, poached, or pan-seared — placed over a bed of mixed salad greens together with a whole or sliced egg and a customer's choice of four additional toppings, which may include vegetables such as cherry tomatoes, cucumber, corn, shredded carrot, beetroot, or bell pepper, finished with a dressing of choice. The dish is minimally processed and not deep-fried. Nutritionally, it provides 84 kcal per 100 g with a moderate protein content of 5.3 g, total fat of 5.57 g, and a low carbohydrate content of 3.24 g, making it one of the lower-calorie and lower-carbohydrate options in the Singapore hawker landscape. Dietary fibre at 1.78 g per 100 g and calcium at 33 mg per 100 g are notable micronutrient contributions. Sodium at 347 mg per 100 g is the primary clinical caution, largely attributable to the dressing. The estimated glycaemic index is approximately 32 (low), yielding a very low glycaemic load of around 3.1 per standard 300 g serving. This dish is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and weight, provided dressing sodium is controlled. It is also appropriate for those following low-calorie or ketogenic dietary patterns. The combination of fish protein, egg, and diverse vegetable toppings provides a broad micronutrient and antioxidant profile.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010026, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous data and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category, clearly documented. Local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are provided as descriptive translations of the English dish name for multilingual compliance, as this is a Western-style dish served in Singapore without established native-language equivalents.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, full micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this customisable composed salad dish are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for F08010026, and no sufficiently close analogue with a full micronutrient breakdown was identified in USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. These fields are set to -1 per the Veritas sentinel convention for data not available in any approved source. Saturated_fat_g is estimated at 1.2 g per 100 g based on USDA FoodData Central data for analogous mixed salad with fish and egg components; this is an approximation and should be verified against direct laboratory analysis. This dish is a Western-style salad served in Singapore under its English descriptor; Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local-language names provided are descriptive translations of the English name for G6 multilingual compliance, as no established native-language culinary names exist for this dish. Keto dietary tag from input is retained as the dish has 3.24 g carbohydrate per 100 g, consistent with ketogenic dietary thresholds.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Documented all -1 sentinel fields in notes to satisfy G3. Corrected ontology match_type values to 'closest_parent' to satisfy G5.
Iteration 2: Attempted to justify absence of local-language names as Western dish; validator rejected this approach for G6, Q1, and Q8.

Iteration 3: Added mandatory local-language names to local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as descriptive translations of the English dish name, satisfying G6 and Q1. Added all local-language name strings to aliases_by_language and flat aliases array, satisfying Q8. Verified aliases consistency: every string in aliases_by_language appears verbatim in the flat aliases array.
